subUnits.title: 2.1 Addition & Subtraction (Up to 4 Digits)
notes: Addition combines numbers to find the total. Subtraction finds the difference between numbers.
When adding/subtracting up to 4-digit numbers, align digits by place value: Thousands, Hundreds, Tens, Ones.
Carry over when a column sum exceeds 9. Borrow from the next column when the top digit is smaller.
Always check your answer by reversing the operation (add to check subtraction, subtract to check addition).

quiz.explanation: Align by place value and add column by column with carrying.

quiz.q: In 2,105 - 876, what do you do when subtracting 6 from 5 in the ones place?
quiz.explanation: When the top digit is smaller, borrow 10 from the next left column.

quiz.q: What is the place value of the digit 8 in 4,821?

subUnits.title: 2.2 Multiplication & Division with Place Value
notes: Multiplication is repeated addition. Division splits a number into equal parts.
Use place value to multiply/divide larger numbers easily. Multiply each digit by the multiplier, then add place values.
In division, divide from the largest place value first. Bring down the next digit when there's a remainder.
Practice: Use real-life examples like sharing items equally or grouping objects.

quiz.q: If 840 is divided equally among 6 students, how many does each get?
Which place value is divided first in 365 ÷ 5?
quiz.explanation: Division always starts from the largest place value (leftmost digit).
